title: Chippenham Stirling Memorial
crew_summary: 7 killed including pilot Flt Sgt. John Whyte

external_narratives.summary: Imperial War Museums register entry for the Stirling Bomber BK784 crash site memorial at Chippenham, Cambridgeshire. A stone tablet set into the ground near the crash site on private farmland off Badlingham Road commemorates the seven crew who died on 23 May 1944.
key_details: Stone tablet on private farmland off Badlingham Road
Crew names: Aston, Eggbeer, McCrory, Pearce, Sharpen, Walley, Whyte

external_narratives.summary: Roll of Honour entry for Chippenham, Cambridgeshire, including the crew of Stirling BK784 from 90 Squadron who were killed in the crash on 23 May 1944.
key_details: All seven crew listed on local roll of honour

local_contacts.role: Local authority responsible for memorial upkeep

sources: Current coords (52.167, 0.228) ~16km SW near Cambridge urban fringe — not Chippenham village
location_confidence.verification_notes: INACCURATE. Previous coords (52.167, 0.228) ~16km SW of Chippenham village — in Cambridge suburban area, not near Chippenham. Village centre at 52.302, 0.437 used as correction.
